emu-annex.p: This annex defines formal algorithms for behaviours defined by this specification. These algorithms are useful primarily for implementing the specification and validating implementations.
Internal fields are implementation-dependent and must not be accessible outside the implementation. For instance they can be C structure fields, ECMAScript private fields, or a combination of both.
Every object conforming to a Class Pattern is expected to have one or several internal fields. This document uses the following operators on internal fields.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. For each internal field of the class being defined, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the internal field. 1. Throw if _object_ has no internal field named _name_.
emu-annex.p: CheckInternalFields throws if an internal field is absent. That can be implicit when internal fields are ECMAScript private fields, or can be explicit when internal fields are C structure fields. The purpose of CheckInternalFields is to ensure that _object_ is an instance of the class being defined.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. For each internal field of the class being defined, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the internal field. 1. Clear the internal field named _name_ of _object_.
emu-annex.p: ClearInternalFields zeroes all internal fields. That can be storing *null* in ECMAScript private fields, or can be storing NULL in C structure fields. The purpose of ClearInternalFields is to ensure that _object_ is in a consistent state when constructed and closed.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Return the value stored in the internal field named _name_ of _object_.
emu-annex.p: GetInternalField is trivial for ECMAScript private fields, but can involve value conversion for C structure field like converting C *NULL* into ECMAScript *null*.

emu-annex.h1: SetInternalField ( _object_: a value, _name_: a value, _value_: a value )
emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Store _value_ in the internal field named _name_ of _object_.
emu-annex.p: SetInternalField is trivial for ECMAScript private fields, but can involve value conversion for C structure field like converting ECMAScript *null* into C *NULL*.

Internal methods are implementation-dependent and must not be accessible outside the implementation. This document uses ECMAScript private method syntax to indicate internal methods, prefixing the names of internal methods with *#*.
For boolean ranges, the value is converted into an ECMAScript boolean.
For number ranges, the value is converted into an ECMAScript number, then the value is checked to be in range. The special value *NaN* is never in range.
For integer ranges, the value is converted into an ECMAScript number, then the value is checked to be an integer, then the value is checked to be in range.
Further restrictions are specified with from x to y, meaning the value must be >= x and <= y.
For object ranges like *ArrayBuffer*, the value is checked to be an instance of one of specified class.
Further restrictions can be specified, for instance on the *byteLength* of the *ArrayBuffer* instance.
If the object can be *null*, it is explicitly specified like *Function* or *null*.
For byte buffer ranges, the value is checked to be an instance of *ArrayBuffer*, *SharedArrayBuffer*, *Uint8Array*, *Int8Array* or *DataView*.
Further restrictions can be specified, for instance on the *byteLength*.
To access the data contained in a byte buffer, algorithms uses a host specific operator:

The operator throws if _buffer_ is not an instance of *ArrayBuffer*, *SharedArrayBuffer*, *Uint8Array*, *Int8Array*, or *DataView*, or if _buffer_ is detached. For a *TypedArray* and *DataView* instances, the pointer takes the view's byte offset into account.
For string ranges like *"buffer"*, the value is converted into an ECMAScript string, then checked to be strictly equal to one of the specified values.
Asynchronous operations are never synchronous: the callback is never invoked directly by the method that starts the asynchronous operation, but indirectly at the end of the asynchronous operation.
To emphasize such a rule, the algorithms uses steps like:
emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*).
emu-annex.p: The mechanism can be similar to what is necessary to implement *setTimeout*.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. ClearInternalFields(*this*). 1. Throw if _options_ is not an object. 1. Let _params_ be an empty object. 1. For each supported option, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the supported option. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, _name_), then 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_options_, _name_). 1. Throw if _value_ is not in the valid range of the supported option. 1. Else, 1. Throw if the supported option has no default value. 1. Let _value_ be the default value of the supported option. 1. DefineProperty(_params_, _name_, _value_). 1. For each supported callback option, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the supported callback option. 1. Let _callback_ be GetProperty(_params_, _name_). 1. If _callback_ is not *null*, then 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. SetInternalField(*this*, _name_, _callback_). 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_params_, *"target"*). 1. If _value_ is not *undefined*, then 1. DefineProperty(*this*, *"target"*, _value_). 1. Mark *this* as ineligible for garbage collection.

ul.li: Supported options, with their names, default values and valid ranges, are defined by a separate table for each class conforming to the Base Class Pattern.

Most classes conforming to the Base Class Pattern are expected to support one or several callbacks. Callbacks are supported options: their default value is *null*, their valid range is *null* or an ECMAScript function. Callbacks are stored in internal fields and are always called with *this* set to the constructed object.
There is only one option that is always supported: its name is *"target"*, its default value is *undefined* and its range is any ECMAScript value.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Mark *this* as eligible for garbage collection. 1. Cancel any pending callbacks for *this*. 1. ClearInternalFields(*this*).

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Throw if _callback_ is not *undefined* and not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. Optionally, cancel asynchronous operations. 1. When all asynchronous operations succeeded or failed: 1. Mark *this* as eligible for garbage collection. 1. ClearInternalFields(*this*). 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, *null*).

1. Execute steps 1 to 7 of the Base Class Pattern *constructor*. 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_params_, *"format"*). 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"format"*, _value_). 1. Try: 1. Let _resources_ be the hardware resources specified by _params_. 1. Throw if _resources_ are unavailable. 1. Allocate and configure _resources_. 1. Throw if allocation or configuration failed. 1. SetInternalField(this, *"resources"*, _resources_). 1. Catch _exception_: 1. Call(GetProperty(*this*, *"close"*), *this*). 1. Throw _exception_. 1. Execute step 8 of the Base Class Pattern *constructor*.

1. Execute step 1 of the Base Class Pattern *close* method. 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Return if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Execute steps 2 and 3 of the Base Class Pattern *close* method. 1. Free _resources_. 1. Execute step 4 of the Base Class Pattern *close* method.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. If _resources_ is not readable, then 1. Return *undefined*. 1. Let _format_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"format"*). 1. If _format_ is *"buffer"*, then 1. Let _available_ be the number of readable bytes. 1. If _option_ is absent, then 1. Throw if _available_ is *undefined*. 1. Let _n_ be _available_. 1. Let _data_ be Construct(*"ArrayBuffer"*, _n_). 1. Let _pointer_ be GetBytePointer(_data_). 1. Read _n_ bytes from _resources_ into _pointer_. 1. Return _data_. 1. Else if _option_ is a number, then 1. Throw if _option_ is no positive integer. 1. Let _n_ be _option_. 1. If _available_ is not *undefined* and _n_ > _available_, then 1. Let _n_ be _available_. 1. Let _data_ be Construct(*"ArrayBuffer"*, _n_). 1. Let _pointer_ be GetBytePointer(_data_). 1. Read _n_ bytes from _resources_ into _pointer_. 1. Return _data_. 1. Else, 1. Let _pointer_ be GetBytePointer(_option_). 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_option_, *"byteLength"*). 1. If _available_ is not *undefined* and _n_ > _available_, then 1. Let _n_ be _available_. 1. Read _n_ bytes from _resources_ into _pointer_. 1. Return _n_. 1. Throw if _option_ is present. 1. Read _data_ from _resources_. 1. Format _data_ according to _format_. 1. Return _data_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null* or not writable. 1. Throw if _data_ is absent. 1. Let _format_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"format"*). 1. If _format_ is *"buffer"*, then 1. Let _pointer_ be GetBytePointer(_data_). 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_data_, *"byteLength"*). 1. Throw if _n_ bytes would overflow _resources_. 1. Write _n_ bytes from _pointer_ into _resources_. 1. Return. 1. Throw if _data_ is not formatted according to _format_. 1. Write _data_ into _resources_.
1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Throw if _value_ is not in the valid range of *"format"*. 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"format"*, _value_).
1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Return GetInternalField(*this*, *"format"*).

ul.li: Hardware resources can require one or several internal fields which should be all cleared and checked. The *"resources"* internal field is only a convention in this document.
Several IO classes read/write bytes into/from buffers so the *read* and *write* methods detail the relevant steps, for instance to optimize the *read* method memory usage by passing a buffer.
IO classes that do not use buffers can skip steps 6 of the *read* and *write* methods.
The ranges of *read* and *write* _data_ are defined by a separate table for each class conforming to the IO Class Pattern.
When the parameters of *read* or *write* differ from the IO Class Pattern, they are defined by a separate table.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute step 1 of the Base Class Pattern *close* method. 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Return if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Optionally, cancel asynchronous operations. 1. When all asynchronous operations succeeded or failed: 1. Mark *this* as eligible for garbage collection. 1. ClearInternalFields(*this*). 1. Free _resources_. 1. Execute step 5.2 and 5.3 of the Base Class Pattern *close* method.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null* or not readable. 1. Throw if _option_ is absent. 1. If _option_ is a number, then 1. Throw if _option_ is no positive integer. 1. Let _n_ be _option_. 1. Let _data_ be Construct(*"ArrayBuffer"*, _n_). 1. Else, 1. Let _data_ be _option_. 1. Let _pointer_ be GetBytePointer(_data_). 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_data_, *"byteLength"*). 1. Throw if _callback_ is not *undefined* and not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. Start an input operation to read _n_ bytes into _data_: 1. When the input operation succeeded: 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, *null*, _data_, _n_). 1. When the input operation failed: 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Let _error_ be an ECMAScript *Error* object describing the failure. 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, _error_).

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null* or not writable. 1. Throw if _data_ is absent. 1. Let _pointer_ be GetBytePointer(_data_). 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_data_, *"byteLength"*). 1. Throw if _callback_ is not *undefined* and not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. Start an output operation to write _n_ bytes from _data_: 1. When the output operation succeeded: 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, *null*, _data_, _n_). 1. When the output operation failed: 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Let _error_ be an ECMAScript *Error* object describing the failure. 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, _error_).

ul.li: The input and output operations represent the implementation dependent mechanism that ensures that asynchronous read and write operations happen in the order issued.
Step 4 of the *close* method is optional since operations can be cancellable or not. Cancelled operations fail with a corresponding *Error* object.
Step 6.2 of the *read* method and step 5 of the *write* method ensures _data_ is a byte buffer.
If the *onReadable* option is not *null*, *edge* is required to have a non-zero value.
Both *rises* and *falls* cannot be *0*; at least one pin must be selected.
The number of readable bytes is undefined so _option_ is required

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null* or not writable or not readable. 1. Let _pointerOut_ be GetBytePointer(_data_). 1. Let _nOut_ be GetProperty(_data_, *"byteLength"*). 1. If _option_ is a number, then 1. Throw if _option_ is no positive integer. 1. Let _buffer_ be Construct(*"ArrayBuffer"*, _option_). 1. Else, 1. Let _buffer_ be _option_. 1. Let _pointerIn_ be GetBytePointer(_buffer_). 1. Let _nIn_ be GetProperty(_buffer_, *"byteLength"*). 1. If _stop_ is absent, then 1. Let _stop_ be *true*. 1. Convert _stop_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Performs atomically: 1. Write _nOut_ bytes from _pointerOut_ into _resources_ with stop bit _stop_. 1. Read _nIn_ bytes from _resources_ into _pointerIn_ with stop bit *1*. 1. Return _buffer_.

1. Execute steps 1 to 7 of the IO.Async Class Pattern *read* method. 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. Convert _stop_ to an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Else if _stop_ is not *undefined*, then 1. If IsCallable(_stop_), then 1. Let _callback_ be _stop_. 1. Let _stop_ be true. 1. Else, 1. Convert _stop_ to an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Else, 1. Let _stop_ be true. 1. Execute step 8 of the IO.Async Class Pattern *read* method.

1. Execute steps 1 to 6 of the IO.Async Class Pattern *write* method. 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. Convert _stop_ to an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Else if _stop_ is not *undefined*, then 1. If IsCallable(_stop_), then 1. Let _callback_ be _stop_. 1. Let _stop_ be true. 1. Else, 1. Convert _stop_ to an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Else, 1. Let _stop_ be true. 1. Execute step 8 of the IO.Async Class Pattern *write* method.

ul.li: The *read* and *write* methods algorithms describe how to handle an optional argument before the optional _callback_ argument.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute steps 1 to 7 of of the I²C – synchronous IO writeRead method. 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. Convert _stop_ to an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Else if _stop_ is not *undefined*, then 1. If IsCallable(_stop_), then 1. Let _callback_ be _stop_. 1. Let _stop_ be true. 1. Else, 1. Convert _stop_ to an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Else, 1. Let _stop_ be true. 1. Convert _stop_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Start an operation that performs atomically: 1. Write _nOut_ bytes from _pointerOut_ into _resources_ with stop bit _stop_. 1. Read _nIn_ bytes from _resources_ into _pointerIn_ with stop bit *1*. 1. When the operation succeeded: 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, *null*, _buffer_, _nOut_). 1. When the operation failed: 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Let _error_ be an ECMAScript *Error* object describing the failure. 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, _error_).

emu-annex.p: All properties from I²C plus the following:

ul.li: The number of readable bytes is undefined so _option_ is required

emu-annex.p: All properties from I²C.Async plus the following:

ul.li: The asynchronous methods to read and write data behaves analogously to the I2C.Async *read* and *write* method.
A host may require the *receive* and/or *transmit* properties.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. If _input_ and _output_ are absent, then 1. Let _flushInput_ be *true*. 1. Let _flushOutput_ be *true*. 1. Else if _input_ and _output_ are present, then 1. Convert _input_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Let _flushInput_ be _input_. 1. Convert _output_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Let _flushOutput_ be _output_. 1. Else, 1. Throw. 1. If _flushInput_ is *true*, then 1. Flush all received but unread data. 1. If _flushOutput_ is *true*, then 1. Flush all written but unsent data.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Throw if _options_ is not an object. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"dataTerminalReady"*), then 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"dataTerminalReady"*). 1. Convert _value_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. If _value_ is *true*, set serial connection's DTR pin. 1. Else clear serial connection's DTR pin. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"requestToSend"*), then 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"requestToSend"*). 1. Convert _value_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. If _value_ is *true*, set serial connection's RTS pin. 1. Else clear serial connection's RTS pin. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"break"*), then 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"break"*). 1. Convert _value_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. If _value_ is *true*, set serial connection's break signal. 1. Else clear serial connection's break signal.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. If _options_ is absent, then 1. Let _result_ be an empty object. 1. Else, 1. Throw if _options_ is not an object. 1. Let _result_ be _options_. 1. If serial connection's CTS pin is set, then 1. SetProperty(_result_, *"clearToSend"*, *true*). 1. Else, 1. SetProperty(_result_, *"clearToSend"*, *false*). 1. If serial connection's DSR pin is set, then 1. SetProperty(_result_, *"dataSetReady"*, *true*). 1. Else, 1. SetProperty(_result_, *"dataSetReady"*, *false*). 1. Return _result_.

ul.li: The number of readable bytes is undefined so _option_ is required

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. If _buffer_ is an ArrayBuffer, then 1. Let _transferBuffer_ be _buffer_. 1. Let _transferOffset_ be 0. 1. Else, 1. Let _transferBuffer_ be GetProperty(_buffer_, "buffer"). 1. Let _transferOffset_ be GetProperty(_buffer_, "byteOffset"). 1. If HasProperty(_buffer_, "bitLength"), then 1. Let _transferBits_ be GetProperty(_buffer_, "bitLength"). 1. Let _availableBits_ be GetProperty(_buffer_, "byteLength") * 8. 1. Throw if _transferBits_ is greater than _availableBits_. 1. Else, 1. Let _transferBits_ be GetProperty(_buffer_, "byteLength") * 8. 1. Simultaneously write and read _transferBits_ bits into _buffer_ starting at byte offset _transferOffset_. 1. Return _buffer_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Flush all written but unsent data. 1. If _deselect_ is present, then 1. Convert _deselect_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. If _deselect_ is *true*, then 1. If GetInternalField(*this*, *"active"*) is 0, then 1. Set the select pin to 1. 1. Else, 1. Set the select pin to 0.
1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Return the local port the listener is bound to as a number.

emu-annex.p: | *onError* | no | *null* or *Function* | *null* | *onWritable* | no | *null* or *Function* | *null* | *format* | no | *"buffer"* | *"buffer"*

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Convert _multicastAddress_ into an ECMAScript string. 1. Throw if _multicastAddress_ is not a valid IP address. 1. Join the multicast group specified by _multicastAddress_. 1. Throw if the operation failed.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Convert _multicastAddress_ into an ECMAScript string. 1. Throw if _multicastAddress_ is not a valid IP address. 1. Leave the multicast group specified by _multicastAddress_. 1. Throw if the operation failed.

1. Execute steps 1 to 7 of the Base Class Pattern *constructor*. 1. Try: 1. For each supported IO connection, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the supported IO connection. 1. Let _ioOptions_ be GetProperty(_params_, _name_). 1. Let _ioConstructor_ be GetProperty(_ioOptions_, *"io"*). 1. Let _ioConnection_ be Construct(_ioConstructor_, _ioOptions_). 1. SetInternalField(*this*, _name_, _ioConnection_). 1. Configure the peripheral with _params_. 1. Throw if the communication with the peripheral is not operational. 1. Activate the peripheral. 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*, *"ready"*). 1. Catch _exception_: 1. Call(GetProperty(*this*, *"close"*), *this*). 1. Throw _exception_. 1. Execute step 8 of the Base Class Pattern *constructor*.

1. Execute step 1 of the Base Class Pattern *close* method. 1. Let _status_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*). 1. Return if _status_ is *null*. 1. Execute steps 2 and 3 of the Base Class Pattern *close* method. 1. Deactivate the peripheral. 1. For each supported IO connection, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the supported IO connection. 1. Let _ioConnection_ be GetInternalField(*this*, _name_). 1. If _ioConnection_ is not *null*, then 1. Call(GetProperty(_ioConnection_, *"close"*), _ioConnection_). 1. Execute step 4 of the Base Class Pattern *close* method.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _status_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*). 1. Throw if _status_ is *null*. 1. Throw if _options_ is *undefined* or *null*. 1. For each supported option, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the supported option. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, _name_), then 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_options_, _name_). 1. Throw if _value_ is not in the valid range of the supported option. 1. Configure the peripheral with _options_.

ul.li: Supported IO connections are supported options. Their value must be an object with an *io* property, which is the class of the IO connection.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute all steps of the Peripheral Class Pattern *constructor*.
1. Execute all steps of the Peripheral Class Pattern *close* method.
1. Execute all steps of the Peripheral Class Pattern *configure* method.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _status_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*). 1. Throw if _status_ is *null*. 1. Throw if _params_ are absent but required, or present but not in the valid range. 1. If the peripheral is readable, then 1. Let _result_ be an empty object. 1. For each sample property, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the sample property. 1. Let _value_ be *undefined*. 1. Read from the peripheral into _value_. 1. DefineProperty(_result_, _name_, _value_). 1. Else, 1. Let _result_ be *undefined*. 1. Return _result_.

ul.li: The order, requirements and ranges of *sample* _params_ are defined by a separate table for each class conforming to the Sensor Class Pattern.
The requirements and ranges of properties in *sample* _result_ are defined by a separate table for each class conforming to the Sensor Class Pattern.

emu-annex.p: None

tr.td: acceleration along the x axis in meters per second squared
acceleration along the y axis in meters per second squared
acceleration along the z axis in meters per second squared

emu-annex.p: None
None
None

tr.td: carbon dioxide in parts per million

emu-annex.p: None

tr.td: carbon monoxide in parts per million

emu-annex.p: None

tr.td: dust levels in micrograms per cubic meter

emu-annex.p: None

tr.td: angular velocity around the x axis in radian per second
angular velocity around the y axis in radian per second
angular velocity around the z axis in radian per second

emu-annex.p: None
None
None

tr.td: hydrogen sulfide in parts per million

emu-annex.p: None

tr.td: magnetic field around the x axis in microtesla
magnetic field around the y axis in microtesla
magnetic field around the z axis in microtesla

emu-annex.p: None
None

tr.td: nitric oxide in parts per million

emu-annex.p: None

tr.td: nitric dioxide in parts per million

emu-annex.p: None
None

tr.td: particulate matter levels in micrograms per cubic meter

emu-annex.p: None

tr.td: indicator of a detected proximate object
distance to the nearest sensed object in centimeters or *null* if no object is detected
maximum sensing range of the sensor in centimeters

emu-annex.p: None
None
None
*Array* of *touch* objects or *undefined* if no touch is in progress.

tr.td: X coordinate of the touch point
Y coordinate of the touch point
indicator of which touch point this entry corresponds to

emu-annex.p: None

tr.td: total volatile organic compounds in parts per billion

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute all steps of the Peripheral Class Pattern *constructor*.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Throw if _area_ is absent. 1. If HasProperty(_area_, *"x"*), then 1. Let _x_ be GetProperty(_area_, *"x"*). 1. Else, 1. Let _x_ be *0*. 1. If HasProperty(_area_, *"y"*), then 1. Let _y_ be GetProperty(_area_, *"y"*). 1. Else, 1. Let _y_ be *0*. 1. If HasProperty(_area_, *"width"*), then 1. Let _width_ be GetProperty(_area_, *"width"*). 1. Else, 1. Let _width_ be the width of the frame buffer in pixels. 1. If HasProperty(_area_, *"height"*), then 1. Let _height_ be GetProperty(_area_, *"height"*). 1. Else, 1. Let _height_ be the height of the frame buffer in pixels. 1. Adjust _x_, _y_, _width_, _height_ to define a valid area to update. 1. SetProperty(_area_, *"x"*, _x_). 1. SetProperty(_area_, *"y"*, _y_). 1. SetProperty(_area_, *"width"*, _width_). 1. SetProperty(_area_, *"height"*, _height_).
1. Execute all steps of the Peripheral Class Pattern *close* method.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _status_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*). 1. Throw if _status_ is *null*. 1. Let _x_ be *0*. 1. Let _y_ be *0*. 1. Let _width_ be the width of the frame buffer in pixels. 1. Let _height_ be the height of the frame buffer in pixels. 1. Let _continue_ be *false*. 1. If _options_ is present, then 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"x"*), then 1. Let _x_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"x"*). 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"y"*), then 1. Let _y_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"y"*). 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"width"*), then 1. Let _width_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"width"*). 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"height"*), then 1. Let _height_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"height"*). 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"continue"*), then 1. Let _continue_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"continue"*). 1. Throw if the area defined by _x_, _y_, _width_, and _height_ is invalid. 1. If _status_ is *ready*, then 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*, *"updating"*). 1. Else, 1. Throw if _continue_ is false. 1. Use _x_, _y_, _width_, _height_ to prepare the frame buffer to receive scanlines.
1. Execute all steps of the Peripheral Class Pattern *configure* method.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _status_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*). 1. Throw if _status_ is not *"updating"*. 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*, *"finishing"*). 1. Make updated frame buffer visible. 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*, *"ready"*).

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _status_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*). 1. Throw if _status_ is not *"updating"*. 1. Throw if _scanlines_ is absent. 1. Let _pointer_ be GetBytePointer(_scanlines_). 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_lines_, *"byteLength"*). 1. Transfer _n_ bytes from _pointer_ to the frame buffer.
1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Return the width of the frame buffer in pixels.
1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Return the height of the frame buffer in pixels.

ul.li: When the frame buffer *rotation* is 90 or 270 degrees, *get width* returns the height of the frame buffer in pixels and *get height* returns the width of the frame buffer in pixels.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute step 1 of the Peripheral Class Pattern *constructor*. 1. Let _interrupt_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"interrupt"*). 1. Let _onAlarm_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"onAlarm"*). 1. If _interrupt_ is not *null* and _onAlarm_ is not *null*, then 1. Let _interruptParams_ be GetProperty(_params_, *"interrupt"*). 1. Let _onReadable_ be a function with the following steps:: 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_onAlarm_, *this*). 1. SetProperty(_interruptParams_, *"onReadable"*, _onReadable_). 1. Execute steps 2 to 4 of the Peripheral Class Pattern *constructor*.
1. Execute all steps of the Peripheral Class Pattern *close* method.
1. Execute all steps of the Peripheral Class Pattern *configure* method.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _status_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"status"*). 1. Throw if _status_ is *null*. 1. If the peripheral is writable, then 1. Convert _time_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Set the clock time to _time_.
1. Execute all steps of the Base Class Pattern *constructor*.
1. Execute all steps of the Base Class Pattern *close* method.

Let _port_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"port"*). 1. Let _onChanged_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"onChanged"*). 1. Monitor the network interface specified by _port_: 1. When changed: 1. If _onChanged_ is not *null*, then 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_onChanged_, *this*).
1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _connection_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"connection"*). 1. If _connection_ is not 0, then 1. Disconnect the network interface.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _connection_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"connection"*). 1. Return _connection_.

1. Execute steps 1 to 6 of the Network Interface Class Pattern *connect* method. 1. Start connecting the network interface specified by _port_. 1. Execute step 7 of the Network Interface Class Pattern *connect* method.

1. Execute steps 1 to 6 of the Network Interface Class Pattern *connect* method. 1. Throw if _options_ is not an object. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"SSID"*), then 1. Let _SSID_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"SSID"*). 1. Convert _SSID_ into an ECMAScript string. 1. Else, 1. Let _SSID_ be *undefined*. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"BSSID"*), then 1. Let _BSSID_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"BSSID"*). 1. Convert _BSSID_ into an ECMAScript string. 1. Else, 1. Let _BSSID_ be *undefined*. 1. Throw if both _SSID_ and _BSSID_ are *undefined*. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"channel"*), then 1. Let _channel_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"channel"*). 1. Convert _channel_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Else, 1. Let _channel_ be *undefined*. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"secure"*), then 1. Let _secure_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"secure"*). 1. Convert _secure_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Else, 1. Let _secure_ be false. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"password"*), then 1. Let _password_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"password"*). 1. Convert _password_ into an ECMAScript string. 1. Else, 1. Let _password_ be *undefined*. 1. Start connecting the network interface specified by _port_ to the access point specified by _SSID_, _BSSID_, _channel_ and _secure_ with _password_. 1. Execute step 7 of the Network Interface Class Pattern *connect* method.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _scanning_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"scanning"*). 1. Throw if _scanning_ is true. 1. Throw if _options_ is not an object. 1. Let _onFound_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"onFound"*). 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_onFound_ ). 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"onComplete"*), then 1. Let _onComplete_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"onComplete"*). 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_onComplete_). 1. Else, 1. Let _onComplete_ be *undefined*. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"channel"*), then 1. Let _channel_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"channel"*). 1. Convert _channel_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Else, 1. Let _channel_ be *undefined*. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"frequency"*), then 1. Let _frequency_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"frequency"*). 1. Convert _frequency_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Throw if _frequency_ is neither 2.4 nor 5. 1. Else, 1. Let _frequency_ be *undefined*. 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"secure"*), then 1. Let _secure_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"secure"*). 1. Convert _secure_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Else, 1. Let _secure_ be false. 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"scanning"*, true). 1. Start scanning for access points matching _channel_, _frequency_ and _secure_: 1. When an access point is found: 1. Let _result_ be an empty object. 1. Let _value_ be the SSID of the access point as an ECMAScript string. 1. SetProperty(_result_, *"SSID"*, _value_). 1. Let _value_ be the BSSID of the access point as a MAC address ECMAScript string. 1. SetProperty(_result_, *"BSSID"*, _value_). 1. Let _value_ be the RSSI of the access point as an ECMAScript number. 1. SetProperty(_result_, *"RSSI"*, _value_). 1. Let _value_ be the channel of the access point as an ECMAScript number. 1. SetProperty(_result_, *"channel"*, _value_). 1. Let _security_ be the security mode of the access point as an ECMAScript string. 1. SetProperty(_security_, *"security"*, _value_). 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_onFound_, *null*, *this*, _result_). 1. When done: 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"scanning"*, false). 1. If _onComplete_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_onComplete_, *this*).

1. Execute all steps of the Base Class Pattern *close* method.

Convert _name_ to an ECMAScript string. 1. Else, 1. Throw. 1. Throw if _callback_ is not *undefined* and not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. If _name_ matches an IP address, then 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, *null*, _name_, _name_). 1. Else, 1. Start the resolution with _name_: 1. When the resolution succeeded: 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Let _address_ be the resolved address as an ECMAScript string. 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, *null*, _name_, _address_). 1. When the resolution failed: 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Let _error_ be an ECMAScript *Error* object describing the failure. 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, _error_).

1. Execute all steps of the Base Class Pattern *close* method.

SetInternalField(*this*, *"synchronizing"*, true). 1. Start the synchronization: 1. When the synchronization succeeded: 1. Let _time_ be the synchronized time as an ECMAScript number. 1. emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute all steps of the Base Class Pattern *constructor*. 1. Let _dnsOptions_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"dns"*). 1. Let _dnsConstructor_ be GetProperty(_dnsOptions_, *"io"*). 1. Let _dnsParams_ be a copy of _dnsOptions_. 1. SetProperty(_dnsParams_, *"target"*, *this*). 1. Let _dnsResolver_ be Construct(_dnsConstructor_, _dnsParams_). 1. SetInternalField(_target_, *"dnsResolver"*, _dnsResolver_). 1. Let _resolve_ be GetProperty(_dnsResolver_, *"resolve"*). 1. Let _resolveParams_ be a new object. 1. SetProperty(_resolveParams_, *"host"*, GetInternalField(*this*, *"host"*)). 1. Let _resolveCallback_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resolveCallback"*). 1. Call(_resolve_, _dnsResolver_, _resolveParams_, _resolveCallback_).

1. Let _tcpSocket_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"tcpSocket"*). 1. If _tcpSocket_ is not *null*, then 1. Call(GetProperty(_tcpSocket_, *"close"*), _tcpSocket_). 1. Let _dnsResolver_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"dnsResolver"*). 1. If _dnsResolver_ is not *null*, then 1. Call(GetProperty(_dnsResolver_, *"close"*), _dnsResolver_). 1. Execute all steps of the Base Class Pattern *close* method.

1. Let _target_ be GetProperty(*this*, *"target"*). 1. Call(GetProperty(*this*, *"close"*), *this*). 1. SetInternalField(_target_, *"dnsResolver"*, *null*). 1. If _error_ is *null*, then 1. Let _tcpOptions_ be GetInternalField(_target_, *"socket"*). 1. Let _tcpConstructor_ be GetProperty(_tcpOptions_, *"io"*). 1. Let _tcpParams_ be a copy of _tcpOptions_. 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"address"*, _address_). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"port"*, GetInternalField(_target_, *"port"*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"onError"*, GetInternalField(*this*, *#tcpError*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"onReadable"*, GetInternalField(*this*, *#tcpReadable*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"onWritable"*, GetInternalField(*this*, *#tcpWritable*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"target"*, *this*). 1. Let _tcpSocket_ be Construct(_tcpConstructor_, _tcpParams_). 1. SetInternalField(_target_, *"tcpSocket"*, _tcpSocket_). 1. Else, 1. Let _onError_ be GetInternalField(_target_, *"onError"*). 1. If _onError_ is not *null*, then 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_onError_, _target_, _error_).

ul.li: The *read*, *write*, *#tcpError*, *#tcpReadable*, *#tcpWritable* functions implement the network protocol, which usually requires a state machine, buffers, parsers, serializers, etc.
Such methods can read and write from the TCP socket and can queue tasks to call the client callbacks.
For each network protocol, the client has specific methods and callbacks, and the *write* method can have specific options.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute step 1 of the TCP Client Class Pattern *constructor*. 1. Let _requests_ be a new *Array* object. 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"requests"*, _requests_). 1. Execute steps 2 to 12 of the TCP Client Class Pattern *constructor*.
1. Let _requests_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"requests"*). 1. For each element _request_ of _requests_, do 1. Cancel _request_. 1. Execute all steps TCP Client Class Pattern *close* method.

1. Let _requests_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"requests"*). 1. Let _requestConstructor_ be the HTTP Client Request constructor. 1. Let _requestParams_ be a copy of _options_. 1. SetProperty(_requestParams_, *"target"*, *this*). 1. Let _request_ be Construct(_requestConstructor_, _requestParams_). 1. Add _request_ to _requests_. 1. When *this* is ready: 1. Start the _request_.

ul.li: The HTTP Client Request constructor is available only to the HTTP Client class.

The HTTP Client class conforms to the TCP Client Class Pattern here above except:
The *read* and *write* methods are provided by the HTTP Client Request instance.
The HTTP Client Request instance owns the network protocol specific callbacks.
If the HTTP Client handles a single request at time, step 7 of the *request* method waits for the former request to complete.
For details about the implementation of the HTTP Client, see the sample code
The *will* object has:
*topic* is required when *operation* is *MQTTClient.PUBLISH*
*QoS*, *retain*, *duplicate*, *byteLength* are used when *operation* is *MQTTClient.PUBLISH*
*items* is required and used when *operation* is *MQTTClient.SUBSCRIBE* or *MQTTClient.UNSUBSCRIBE*.
*items* is an array of objects that have:
*QoS* is used when *operation* is *MQTTClient.SUBSCRIBE*
The MQTT Client class conforms to the TCP Client Class Pattern here above.
For details about the implementation of the MQTT Client, see the sample code

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute step 1 of the TCP Client Class Pattern *constructor*. 1. Let _tcpSocket_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"attach"*). 1. If _tcpSocket_ is *null*, then 1. Execute steps 2 to 12 of the TCP Client Class Pattern *constructor*. 1. Else, 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"tcpSocket"*, _tcpSocket_).

ul.li: If *attach* is present, *dns*, *socket*, *host*, *port*, *path*, *protocol* and *headers* are neither required nor used.
The WebSocket Client class conforms to the TCP Client Class Pattern here above.
For details about the implementation of the WebSocket Client, see the sample code

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute all steps of the Base Class Pattern *constructor*. 1. Let _connections_ be a new *Set* object. 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"connections"*, _connections_). 1. Let _tcpOptions_ be GetInternalField(_target_, *"listener"*). 1. Let _tcpConstructor_ be GetProperty(_tcpOptions_, *"io"*). 1. Let _tcpParams_ be a copy of _tcpOptions_. 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"port"*, GetInternalField(_target_, *"port"*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"onReadable"*, GetInternalField(*this*, *#tcpReadable*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"target"*, *this*). 1. Let _tcpSocket_ be Construct(_tcpConstructor_, _tcpParams_). 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"tcpSocket"*, _tcpSocket_).

1. Let _connections_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"connections"*). 1. For each element _connection_ of _connections_, do 1. Call(GetProperty(_connection_, *"close"*), _connection_). 1. Let _tcpSocket_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"tcpSocket"*). 1. If _tcpSocket_ is not *null*, then 1. Call(GetProperty(_tcpSocket_, *"close"*), _tcpSocket_). 1. Execute all steps of the Base Class Pattern *close* method.

1. Let _target_ be GetProperty(*this*, *"target"*). 1. Let _connections_ be GetInternalField(_target_, *"connections"*). 1. Let _onConnect_ be GetInternalField(_target_, *"onConnect"*). 1. Let _connectionConstructor_ be a class conforming to the TCP Server Connection Class Pattern. 1. While _count_ > 0: 1. Let _from_ be Call(GetProperty(*this*, *"read"*), *this*). 1. Let _connection_ be Construct(_connectionConstructor_, _target_, _from_). 1. Add _connection_ to _connections_. 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Call(_onConnect_, _target_, _connection_). 1. Let _count_ be _count_ - 1.

ul.li: The connection constructor is specific to each network protocol, and available only to the implementation: a static private field of the server class, a closure of the server module, etc.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"server"*, _server_). 1. Let _tcpConstructor_ be GetProperty(_from_, *"constructor"*). 1. Let _tcpParams_ be Construct(GetProperty(*globalThis*, *"Object"*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"from"*, _from_). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"onError"*, GetInternalField(*this*, *#tcpError*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"onReadable"*, GetInternalField(*this*, *#tcpReadable*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"onWritable"*, GetInternalField(*this*, *#tcpWritable*)). 1. SetProperty(_tcpParams_, *"target"*, *this*). 1. Let _tcpSocket_ be Construct(_tcpConstructor_, _tcpParams_). 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"tcpSocket"*, _tcpSocket_).

1. Let _tcpSocket_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"tcpSocket"*). 1. If _tcpSocket_ is not *null*, then 1. Call(GetProperty(_tcpSocket_, *"close"*), _tcpSocket_). 1. Let _server_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"server"*). 1. Let _connections_ be GetInternalField(_server_, *"connections"*). 1. Remove *this* from _connections_.

ul.li: The *read*, *write*, *#tcpError*, *#tcpReadable*, *#tcpWritable* functions implement the network protocol, which usually requires a state machine, buffers, parsers, serializers, etc.
Such methods can read and write from the TCP socket and can queue tasks to call the server connection callbacks.
For each network protocol, the server connection has specific methods and callbacks, and the *write* method can have specific options.
The HTTP Server class conforms to the TCP Server Class Pattern here above.
The server connection constructor is the HTTP Server Connection class.
For details about the implementation of the HTTP Server, see the sample code

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Let _tcpSocket_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"tcpSocket"*). 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"tcpSocket"*, *null*). 1. Let _server_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"server"*). 1. Let _connections_ be GetInternalField(_server_, *"connections"*). 1. Remove *this* from _connections_. 1. Return _tcpSocket_.

1. Throw if options is not an object. 1. For each supported callback, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the supported callback. 1. Let _callback_ be GetProperty(_options_, _name_). 1. If _callback_ is not *undefined*, then 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. SetInternalField(*this*, _name_, _callback_). 1. Start receiving the request.
1. Let _result_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"route"*). 1. Return result.
1. Execute steps 1 and 2 of the *accept* method. 1. SetInternalField(*this*, *"route"*, _options_).

1. Throw if options is not an object. 1. Let _status_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"status"*). 1. Convert _status_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Throw if _status_ is no positive integer. 1. Let _headers_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"headers"*). 1. Throw if _headers_ is no *Map* instance. 1. Start sending the response with _status_ and _headers_.

ul.li: The HTTP Server Connection class conforms to the TCP Server Connection Class Pattern here above.
The HTTP Connection callbacks can be changed with the *route* setter, usually in the *onRequest* callback, when the HTTP method, path, and headers are available.
For examples of routes, see the sample code

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Execute all steps of the Sensor Class Pattern configure method.

1. Execute steps 1 to 6 of the Sensor Class Pattern *sample* method. 1. If _result_ is an object, then 1. If an absolute clock is available, then 1. Let _time_ be the value of the absolute clock upon sampling. 1. DefineProperty(_result_, *"time"*, _time_). 1. If a relative clock is available, then 1. Let _ticks_ be the value of a relative clock upon sampling. 1. DefineProperty(_result_, *"ticks"*, _ticks_). 1. If faults are readable from the sensor upon sampling, then 1. Read from the sensor into _faults_. 1. DefineProperty(_result_, *"faults"*, _faults_). 1. Execute steps 7 of the Sensor Class Pattern *sample* method.

ul.li: The absolute clock is the most precise clock available to get an absolute time value (since the Epoch), from either the sensor, the microcontroller, or another peripheral.
The relative clock is any clock available to get a consistent relative time value (for instance since the device started), from either the sensor, the microcontroller, or another peripheral.

emu-annex.p: None
In addition to the sample results defined in the Sensor Class Pattern, the Provenance Sensor Class Pattern adds properties as follows:

tr.td: number originating from an absolute clock describing the instant that the sample returned was captured
number originating from a non-absolute clock describing the instant that the sample returned was captured
object representing a record of any sensor-level faults that occurred during this sensor sample or since the previously reported sample

ul.li: The order, requirements, and ranges of options for configure extend those found in a separate table for every class conforming to the Sensor Class Pattern, and add the options *configuration* and *identification* as defined in the Sensor Provenance Class Pattern.

Metadata (_time_, _ticks_, _faults_) reflect only the metadata associated with the first sample. In cases where multiple samples may be taken from a single device, timing and fault data may be imprecise for subsequent samples.
The Audio Input Class conforms to the IO Class Pattern for its *constructor*, *close* and *read* methods. There is no *write* method.
The *"resources"* internal field of an Audio Input instance represents the hardware and software necessary to capture audio samples on the device.
The *constructor* does not start capturing audio samples. Use the *start* method.
When audio samples are available to read, the *onReadable* callback is invoked with two arguments, _byteLength_ and _sampleCount_.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. If _resources_ already started, then 1. Return. 1. Start capturing audio with _resources_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _flush_ be *false*. 1. If _options_ is provided, then 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"flush"*), then 1. Let _flush_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"flush"*). 1. Convert _flush_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. If _resources_ capturing audio, then 1. Stop capturing audio with _resources_. 1. If _flush_, then 1. Flush unread samples in _resources_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _audioType_ be the encoding of _resources_. 1. Return _audioType_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _bitsPerSample_ be the number of bits per sample of _resources_. 1. Return _bitsPerSample_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _channels_ be the number of channels of _resources_. 1. Return _channels_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _sampleRate_ be the sample rate of _resources_. 1. Return _sampleRate_.

ul.li: The asynchronous version of the Audio Input Class extends the Audio Input Class in order to conform to the asynchronous version of the IO Class Pattern.
The *onReadable* callback is never invoked.
The _callback_ of the *read* method is invoked when audio samples have been read.
The Audio Output Class conforms to the IO Class Pattern for its *constructor*, *close* and *write* methods. There is no *read* method.
The *"resources"* internal field of an Audio Output instance represents the hardware and software necessary to play audio samples on the device.
The *constructor* does not start playing audio samples. Use the *start* method.
When space is available to write audio samples, the *onWritable* callback is invoked with two arguments, _byteLength_ and _sampleCount_.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. If _resources_ already started, then 1. Return. 1. Start playing audio with _resources_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _flush_ be *false*. 1. If _options_ is provided, then 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"flush"*), then 1. Let _flush_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"flush"*). 1. Convert _flush_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. If _resources_ playing audio, then 1. Stop playing audio with _resources_. 1. If _flush_, then 1. Flush unplayed samples in _resources_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _audioType_ be the encoding of _resources_. 1. Return _audioType_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _bitsPerSample_ be the number of bits per sample of _resources_. 1. Return _bitsPerSample_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _channels_ be the number of channels of _resources_. 1. Return _channels_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _sampleRate_ be the sample rate of _resources_. 1. Return _sampleRate_.

ul.li: The asynchronous version of the Audio Output Class extends the Audio Output Class in order to conform to the asynchronous version of the IO Class Pattern.
The *onWritable* callback is never invoked.
The _callback_ of the *write* method is invoked when audio samples have been written.
The Image Input Class conforms to the IO Class Pattern for its *constructor* and *close* method. There is no *write* method.

The *"resources"* internal field of an Image Input instance represents the hardware and software necessary to capture image frames on the device. Typically, an Image Input instance manages a queue of image frames.
The *constructor* does not start capturing image frames. Use the *start* method.
When an image frame is available to read, the *onReadable* callback is invoked.
If the application provides a byte buffer to the *read* method, its byte length must be at least the byte length of a frame.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _format_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"format"*). 1. If _format_ is *"buffer/disposable"*, then 1. If an image frame is available in _resources_, then 1. Let _buffer_ be CreateImageInputBuffer(_resources_). 1. Return _buffer_. 1. Else, 1. Return *undefined*. 1. Else, 1. Execute step 6 to 10 of the IO Class Pattern *read* method.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. If _resources_ already started, then 1. Return. 1. Start capturing image frames with _resources_.

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Let _flush_ be *false*. 1. If _options_ is provided, then 1. If HasProperty(_options_, *"flush"*), then 1. Let _flush_ be GetProperty(_options_, *"flush"*). 1. Convert _flush_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. If _resources_ capturing image frames, then 1. Stop capturing image frames with _resources_. 1. If _flush_, then 1. Flush unread image frames in _resources_.

Set _feature_ of _resources_ to _value_. 1. _setOption_(*"brightness"*). 1. _setOption_(*"contrast"*). 1. _setOption_(*"saturation"*). 1. _setOption_(*"sharpness"*). 1. _setOption_(*"denoise"*). 1. _setOption_(*"whiteBalance"*).

ul.li: The asynchronous version of the Image Input Class extends the Image Input Class in order to conform to the asynchronous version of the IO Class Pattern.
The *onReadable* callback is never invoked.
The _callback_ of the *read* method is invoked when a frame has been read.

Let _format_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"format"*). 1. If _format_ is *"buffer/disposable"*, then 1. Throw if _callback_ is not *undefined* and not IsCallable(_callback_). 1. When an image frame is available in _resources_: 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. Let _buffer_ be CreateImageInputBuffer(_resources_). 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, *null*, _buffer_). 1. Else, 1. Execute step 4 to 8 of the IO Class Pattern – asynchronous *read* method.

ul.li: The Image Input Buffer Prototype conforms to the Disposable Buffer Pattern. Its instances are byte buffers that reference image frames in the Image Input instance.
It is the responsibility of the application to close Image Input Buffer instances as soon as possible to allow the Image Input instance to reuse the referenced image frames.

Let _provider_ be GetProperty(*this*, *"target"*). 1. Dispatch _data_ among IO objects of _provider_. 1. Let _count_ be the number of supported IO connection. 1. Let _onWritable_ be a function with the following steps:: 1. Let _count_ be _count_ - 1. 1. If _count_ is 0, then 1. Let _provider_ be GetProperty(*this*, *"target"*). 1. Configure _provider_ with _params_. 1. Add supported IO constructors to _provider_. 1. SetInternalField(_provider_, *"status"*, *"ready"*). 1. Let _callback_ be GetInternalField(_provider_, *"onReady"*). 1. If _callback_ is not *null*, then 1. Call(_callback_, _provider_). 1. Let _onError_ be a function with the following steps:: 1. Let _provider_ be GetProperty(*this*, *"target"*). 1. Dispatch the error to open IO objects of _provider_. 1. Call(GetProperty(_provider_, *"close"*), _provider_). 1. Let _callback_ be GetInternalField(_provider_, *"onError"*). 1. If _callback_ is not *null*, then 1. Call(_callback_, _provider_). 1. Try: 1. For each supported IO connection, do 1. Let _name_ be the name of the supported IO connection. 1. Let _ioOptions_ be GetProperty(_params_, _name_). 1. Let _ioParams_ be a copy of _ioOptions_. 1. Let _ioConstructor_ be GetProperty(_ioParams_, *"io"*). 1. DefineProperty(_ioParams_, *"onReadable"*, _onReadable_). 1. DefineProperty(_ioParams_, *"onWritable"*, _onWritable_). 1. DefineProperty(_ioParams_, *"onError"*, _onError_). 1. DefineProperty(_ioParams_, *"target"*, *this*). 1. Let _ioConnection_ be Construct(_ioConstructor_, _ioParams_). 1. SetInternalField(*this*, _name_, _ioConnection_). 1. Catch _exception_: 1. Call(GetProperty(*this*, *"close"*), *this*). 1. Throw _exception_. 1. Execute step 8 of the Base Class Pattern *constructor*.
1. Execute all steps of the Peripheral Class Pattern *close* method.

ul.li: The Flash module default export is an object with an *open* method that creates Flash Partition instances.

Let _blocks_ be the number of blocks in _partition_. 1. Convert _from_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. If _to_ is absent, then 1. Let _to_ be _from_ + 1. 1. Else, 1. Let _result_ be Construct(GetProperty(*globalThis*, *"ArrayBuffer"*), _count_). 1. Let _pointer_ be GetBytePointer(_result_). 1. Else, 1. Let _pointer_ be GetBytePointer(_count_). 1. Let _count_ be GetProperty(_count_, *"byteLength"*). 1. If _count_ > _available_, then 1. Let _count_ be _available_. 1. Let _result_ be _count_. 1. Read _count_ bytes into _pointer_ from _partition_ at _offset_. 1. Return _result_.

Set the size of _update_ to _size_. 1. Begin _update_ . 1. SetInternalField(this, *"resources"*, _update_). 1. Execute steps 5 to 6 of the IO Class Pattern *constructor*. 1. Return *this*.

The *"resources"* internal field of a Directory instance describes a specific directory in the file system. On POSIX, the internal field is a file descriptor.
All directory and file entries are specified by a path relative to the specific directory described by the Directory instance resources.
Paths are strings. The path separator is *"/"*. CheckPath enforces paths to be beneath the specific directory described by the Directory instance resources.

ul.li: Closing the iterator stream must also close the resources used to open the iterator stream.
See man closedir
The File module default export is a Directory instance, which is used to access file and directory entries in the file system.
On POSIX, it is typically the *$HOME* directory.

emu-annex.emu-note: The internal hierarchy of services, characteristics, and descriptors is specified here with arrays and objects. But only characteristic and descriptor objects corresponding to variable characteristics and descriptors are observable, thru their callbacks. The rest of the hierarchy can be implemented without arrays and objects. However, observable objects in the hierarchy cannot be garbage collected until their server is closed.

For each element _service_ of _services_, do 1. DeleteGATTService(_service_). 1. Execute all steps of the IO Class Pattern *close* method.

The algorithms also use the following constants:

dl.dd: It returns a service object (an instance of the GATTServerService Class).

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Throw if _serviceRecord_ is not an object. 1. Let _uuid_ be GetProperty(_serviceRecord_, *"uuid"*). 1. Let _uuid_ be UUIDStringToBuffer(_uuid_). 1. Let _service_ be a new instance of the GATTServerService Class. 1. Let _resources_ be a new peripheral service specified by _uuid_. 1. SetInternalField(_service_, *"resources"*, _resources_). 1. If HasProperty(_serviceRecord_, *"characteristics"*), then 1. Let _characteristicRecords_ be GetProperty(_serviceRecord_, *"characteristics"*). 1. Throw if _characteristicRecords_ is not an array. 1. Let _characteristics_ be a new array. 1. For each element _characteristicRecord_ of _characteristicRecords_, do 1. Let _characteristic_ be CreateGATTCharacteristic(_characteristicRecord_). 1. Add _characteristic_ to _characteristics_. 1. Else, 1. Let _characteristics_ be *null*. 1. SetInternalField(_service_, *"characteristics"*, _characteristics_). 1. Return _service_.

1. Let _characteristics_ be GetInternalField(_service_, *"characteristics"*). 1. If _characteristics_ is not *null*, then 1. For each element _characteristic_ of _characteristics_, do 1. DeleteGATTCharacteristic(_characteristic_). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(_service_, *"resources"*). 1. If _resources_ is not *null*, then 1. Free _resources_. 1. ClearInternalFields(_service_).

tr.td: number, a logical OR of GATTServer properties

dl.dd: It returns a characteristic object (an instance of the GATTServerCharacteristic Class).

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Throw if _characteristicRecord_ is not an object. 1. Let _uuid_ be GetProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"uuid"*). 1. Let _uuid_ be UUIDStringToBuffer(_uuid_). 1. Let _properties_ be GetProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"properties"*). 1. Convert _properties_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Throw if _properties_ is not a positive integer. 1. If HasProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"onRead"*), then 1. Let _onRead_ be GetProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"onRead"*). 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_onRead_). 1. Else, 1. Let _onRead_ be *null*. 1. If HasProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"onWrite"*), then 1. Let _onWrite_ be GetProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"onWrite"*). 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_onWrite_). 1. Else, 1. Let _onWrite_ be *null*. 1. If HasProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"onSubscribe"*), then 1. Let _onSubscribe_ be GetProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"onSubscribe"*). 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_onSubscribe_). 1. Else, 1. Let _onSubscribe_ be *null*. 1. If HasProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"onUnsubscribe"*), then 1. Let _onUnsubscribe_ be GetProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"onUnsubscribe"*). 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_onUnsubscribe_). 1. Else, 1. Let _onUnsubscribe_ be *null*. 1. Let _characteristic_ be a new instance of the GATTServerCharacteristic Class. 1. If HasProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"value"*), then 1. Throw if some of _onRead_, _onWrite_, _onSubscribe_, _onUnsubscribe_ are not *null*. 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"value"*). 1. Throw if _value_ is not a readable byte buffer. 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_value_, *"byteLength"*). 1. NOTE: When a connection is established, report a warning if _n_ is bigger than the connection maximumWrite. 1. Let _resources_ be a new constant peripheral service characteristic specified by _uuid_, _properties_ and _value_. 1. Else, 1. If _properties_ *&* READABLE is *0*, then 1. Throw if _onRead_ is not *null*. 1. Else, 1. Throw if _onRead_ is *null*. 1. If _properties_ *&* WRITABLE is *0*, then 1. Throw if _onWrite_ is not *null*. 1. Else, 1. Throw if _onWrite_ is *null*. 1. If _properties_ *&* NOTIFIABLE is *0*, then 1. Throw if _onSubscribe_ is not *null*. 1. Else, 1. Throw if _onSubscribe_ is *null*. 1. NOTE: _characteristic_ is the *this* value when calling _onRead_, _onWrite_, _onSubscribe_, _onUnsubscribe_. 1. SetInternalField(_characteristic_, `"onRead"`, _onRead_). 1. SetInternalField(_characteristic_, `"onWrite"`, _onWrite_). 1. SetInternalField(_characteristic_, `"onSubscribe"`, _onSubscribe_). 1. SetInternalField(_characteristic_, `"onUnsubscribe"`, _onUnsubscribe_). 1. Let _resources_ be a new variable peripheral service characteristic specified by _uuid_, _properties_, and a reference to _characteristic_. 1. SetInternalField(_characteristic_, `"resources"`, _resources_). 1. If HasProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"descriptors"*), then 1. Let _descriptorRecords_ be GetProperty(_characteristicRecord_, *"descriptors"*). 1. Throw if _descriptorRecords_ is not an array. 1. Let _descriptors_ be a new array. 1. For each element _descriptorRecord_ of _descriptorRecords_, do 1. Let _descriptor_ be CreateGATTDescriptor(_descriptorRecord_). 1. Add _descriptor_ to _descriptors_. 1. Else, 1. Let _descriptors_ be *null*. 1. SetInternalField(_characteristic_, `"descriptors"`, _descriptors_). 1. Return _characteristic_.

1. Let _descriptors_ be GetInternalField(_characteristic_, *"descriptors"*). 1. If _descriptors_ is not *null*, then 1. For each element _descriptor_ of _descriptors_, do 1. DeleteGATTDescriptor(_descriptor_). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(_characteristic_, *"resources"*). 1. If _resources_ is not *null*, then 1. Free _resources_. 1. ClearInternalFields(_characteristic_).

dl.dd: It returns a descriptor object (an instance of the GATTServerDescriptor Class).

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. Throw if _descriptorRecord_ is not an object. 1. Let _uuid_ be GetProperty(_descriptorRecord_, *"uuid"*). 1. Let _uuid_ be UUIDStringToBuffer(_uuid_). 1. If HasProperty(_descriptorRecord_, *"onRead"*), then 1. Let _onRead_ be GetProperty(_descriptorRecord_, *"onRead"*). 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_onRead_). 1. Else, 1. Let _onRead_ be *null*. 1. If HasProperty(_descriptorRecord_, *"onWrite"*), then 1. Let _onWrite_ be GetProperty(_descriptorRecord_, *"onWrite"*). 1. Throw if not IsCallable(_onWrite_). 1. Else, 1. Let _onWrite_ be *null*. 1. Let _descriptor_ be a new instance of the GATTServerDescriptor Class. 1. If HasProperty(_descriptorRecord_, *"value"*), then 1. Throw if either _onRead_ or _onWrite_ is not *null*. 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_descriptorRecord_, *"value"*). 1. Throw if _value_ is not a readable byte buffer. 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_value_, *"byteLength"*). 1. NOTE: When a connection is established, report a warning if _n_ is bigger than the connection maximumWrite. 1. Let _resources_ be a new constant peripheral service characteristic descriptor specified by _uuid_ and _value_. 1. Else, 1. Throw if both _onRead_ and _onWrite_ are *null*. 1. NOTE: _descriptor_ is the *this* value when calling _onRead_ or _onWrite_. 1. SetInternalField(_descriptor_, `"onRead"`, _onRead_). 1. SetInternalField(_descriptor_, `"onWrite"`, _onWrite_). 1. Let _resources_ be a new variable peripheral service characteristic descriptor specified by _uuid_ and a reference to _descriptor_. 1. SetInternalField(_descriptor_, `"resources"`, _resources_). 1. Return _descriptor_.
1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(_descriptor_, *"resources"*). 1. If _resources_ is not *null*, then 1. Free _resources_. 1. ClearInternalFields(_descriptor_).

1. Throw if _advertisement_ is not an object. 1. Let _options_ be a new object. 1. DefineProperty(_options_, *"maxByteLength"*, *31*). 1. [declared="ArrayBuffer"] Let _buffer_ be Construct(_ArrayBuffer_, _0_, _options_). 1. [declared="Uint8Array"] Let _view_ be Construct(_Uint8Array_, _buffer_). 1. Let _offset_ be *0*. 1. Let _appendLength_ be a new Abstract Closure with parameters (_length_) that captures _buffer_, _view_, _offset_ and performs the following steps when called: 1. Call(GetProperty(_buffer_, *"resize"*), _buffer_, _offset_ *+* *1* *+* _length_). 1. SetProperty(_view_, _offset_, _length_). 1. Let _offset_ be _offset_ *+* *1*. 1. Let _bufferByte_ be a new Abstract Closure with parameters (_byte_) that captures _buffer_, _view_, _offset_ and performs the following steps when called: 1. SetProperty(_view_, _offset_, _byte_). 1. Let _offset_ be _offset_ *+* *1*. 1. Let _bufferBytes_ be a new Abstract Closure with parameters (_n_, _bytes_) that captures _buffer_, _view_, _offset_ and performs the following steps when called: 1. Call(GetProperty(_view_, *"set"*), _view_, _bytes_, _offset_). 1. Let _offset_ be _offset_ *+* _n_. 1. For each key _key_ in _advertisement_, do 1. Let _value_ be GetProperty(_advertisement_, _key_). 1. If _key_ is *"name"*, then 1. Convert _value_ into a string. 1. Convert _value_ into a byte buffer. 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_value_, *"byteLength"*). 1. _appendLength_(*1* *+* _n_). 1. _bufferByte_(*9*). 1. _bufferBytes_(_n_, _value_). 1. Else if _key_ is *"services"*, then 1. Throw if _value_ is not an array. 1. Let _sorters_ be a new array. 1. Let _addSorter_ be a new Abstract Closure with parameters (_n_, _id_) that captures _sorters_ and performs the following steps when called: 1. Let _sorter_ be a new object. 1. Let _array_ be a new array. 1. DefineProperty(_sorter_, *n*, _n_). 1. DefineProperty(_sorter_, *id*, _id_). 1. DefineProperty(_sorter_, *array*, _array_). 1. Add _sorter_ to _sorters_. 1. _addSorter_(*2*, *3*). 1. _addSorter_(*4*, *5*). 1. _addSorter_(*16*, *7*). 1. For each element _uuid_ of _value_, do 1. Let _uuid_ be UUIDStringToBuffer(_uuid_). 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_uuid_, *"byteLength"*). 1. Let _sorter_ be _sorters_ element matching _n_. 1. Throw if _sorter_ is *undefined*. 1. Let _array_ be GetProperty(_sorter_, "*array*"). 1. Add _uuid_ to _array_. 1. For each element _sorter_ of _sorters, do 1. Let _array_ be GetProperty(_sorter_, "*array*"). 1. If _array_ has elements, then 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_sorter_, "*n*"). 1. _appendLength_(*1* *+* _length_ *×* _n_). 1. _bufferByte_(GetProperty(_sorter_, "*id*")). 1. For each element _uuid_ of _array_, do 1. _bufferBytes_(_n_, _uuid_). 1. Else if _key_ is *"manufacturerData"*, then 1. Let _manufacturer_ be GetProperty(_value_, *"manufacturer"*). 1. Convert _manufacturer_ into a number. 1. Throw if _manufacturer_ is not a 16-bit unsigned integer. 1. Let _data_ be GetProperty(_value_, *"data"*). 1. Throw if _data_ is not a readable byte buffer. 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_data_, *"byteLength"*). 1. _appendLength_(*3* *+* _n_). 1. _bufferByte_(*255*). 1. _bufferByte_(_manufacturer_). 1. _bufferByte_(_manufacturer_ *>>* *8*). 1. _bufferBytes_(_n_, _data_). 1. Else if _key_ is *"flags"*, then 1. Convert _value_ into a number. 1. Throw if _value_ is not an 8-bit unsigned integer. 1. _appendLength_(*2*). 1. _bufferByte_(*1*). 1. _bufferByte_(_value_). 1. Else, 1. Let _index_ be ToIndex(_key_). 1. Throw if _index_ is *undefined* or more than *255*. 1. Throw if _value_ is not a readable byte buffer. 1. Let _n_ be GetProperty(_value_, *"byteLength"*). 1. _appendLength_(*1* *+* _n_). 1. _bufferByte_(_index_). 1. _bufferBytes_(_n_, _value_). 1. Return _buffer_.
1. Throw.

ul.li: The GATT server creates GATTServerConnection instances when centrals connect/disconnect to/from the peripheral.

A GATTServerConnection instance is passed to the *onConnect*, *onDisconnect*, *onPasskey* and *onSecured* server callbacks, to the *onRead*, *onWrite*, *onSubscribe* and *onUnsubscribe* characteristic callbacks, and to the *onRead* and *onWrite* descriptor callbacks.
A GATTServerConnection instance is necessary to notify a GATTServerCharacteristic instance.
A GATTServerConnection instance has a *"server"* internal field to reference its server.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Return if _resources_ is *null*. 1. Close _resources_. 1. Let _server_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"server"*). 1. Let _connections_ be GetInternalField(_server_, *"connections"*). 1. Remove *this* from _connections_. 1. ClearInternalFields(*this*).

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. CheckInternalFields(_characteristic_). 1. Let _characteristicResources_ be GetInternalField(_characteristic_, *"resources"*). 1. Throw if _value_ is not a readable byte buffer. 1. Notify _resources_ with _characteristicResources_ and _value_. 1. When the operation completes: 1. If _callback_ is present, then 1. Queue a task that performs: 1. If the operation failed, then 1. Let _error_ be an ECMAScript *Error* object describing the failure. 1. Else, 1. Let _error_ be *null*. 1. Call(_callback_, *this*, _error_).

1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. If _action_ is *"input"*, then 1. If _data_ is present, then 1. Convert _data_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Else, 1. Let _data_ be *0*. 1. Else if _action_ is *"compareNumber"*, then 1. Convert _data_ into an ECMAScript boolean. 1. Else if _action_ is *"outOfBand"*, then 1. If _data_ is not a readable byte buffer, then 1. Throw. 1. Let _n_ be the byte length of _data_. 1. If _n_ is not *16*, then 1. Throw. 1. Else, 1. Throw. 1. Reply to passkey with _action_ and _data_.
1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _mtu_ be the MTU negotiated between the peripheral and the central. 1. Convert _mtu_ into an ECMAScript number. 1. Return _mtu_ *-* *3*.
1. Throw.
emu-annex.emu-note: The GATT server creates GATTServerService instances when adding services to a peripheral. No GATTServerService instances are observable.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Let _uuid_ be _resources_ UUID as a readable byte buffer. 1. Return UUIDBufferToString(_uuid_).
1. Throw.
emu-annex.emu-note: The GATT server creates GATTServerCharacteristic instances when adding characteristics to a peripheral service. Only GATTServerCharacteristic instances corresponding to variable characteristics are observable thru their callbacks.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Let _uuid_ be _resources_ .[[UUID]] as a readable byte buffer. 1. Return UUIDBufferToString(_uuid_).
1. Throw.
emu-annex.emu-note: The GATT server creates GATTServerDescriptor instances when adding descriptors to peripheral service characteristic. Only GATTServerDescriptor instances corresponding to variable descriptors are observable thru their callbacks.

emu-annex.emu-alg: 1. CheckInternalFields(*this*). 1. Let _resources_ be GetInternalField(*this*, *"resources"*). 1. Let _uuid_ be _resources_ UUID as a readable byte buffer. 1. Return UUIDBufferToString(_uuid_).

1. [declared="ArrayBuffer,Uint8Array"] If Call(GetProperty(_ArrayBuffer_, *"isView"*), _ArrayBuffer_, _buffer_) is *true*, then 1. Let _byteOffset_ be GetProperty(_buffer_, *"byteOffset"*). 1. Let _byteLength_ be GetProperty(_buffer_, *"byteLength"*). 1. Let _buffer_ be GetProperty(_buffer_, *"buffer"*). 1. Let _view_ be Construct(_Uint8Array_, _buffer_, _byteOffset_, _byteLength_). 1. Else, 1. Let _view_ be Construct(_Uint8Array_, _buffer_). 1. Let _string_ be Call(GetProperty(_view_, *"toHex"*), _view_). 1. Let _n_ be the number fo characters in _string_. 1. If _n_ is *32*, then 1. NOTE: Format is xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx. 1. Let _s0_ be _string_ characters from index *0* to *7*. 1. Let _s1_ be _string_ characters from index *8* to *11*. 1. Let _s2_ be _string_ characters from index *12* to *15*. 1. Let _s3_ be _string_ characters from index *16* to *19*. 1. Let _s4_ be _string_ characters from index *20* to *31*. 1. NOTE: Canonicalizes Bluetooth Base UUID. 1. If _s1_ is *"0000"* and _s2_ is *"1000"* and _s3_ is *"8000"* and _s4_ is *"00805f9b34fb"*, then 1. If _s0_ starts with *"0000"*, then 1. Let _string_ be _s0_ characters from index *4* to *7*. 1. Else, 1. Let _string_ be _s0_. 1. Else, 1. Let _string_ be _s0_ *+* *"-"* *+* _s1_ *+* *"-"* *+* _s2_ *+* *"-"* *+* _s3_ *+* *"-"* *+* _s4_. 1. Else if _n_ is neither *4* nor *8*, then 1. Throw. 1. Return _string_.

1. Convert _string_ into an ECMAScript string. 1. Let _n_ be the number fo characters in _string_. 1. If _n_ is *36*, then 1. NOTE: Format is xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx. 1. Let _s0_ be _string_ characters from *0* to *7*. 1. Let _s1_ be _string_ characters from *9* to *12*. 1. Let _s2_ be _string_ characters from *14* to *17*. 1. Let _s3_ be _string_ characters from *19* to *22*. 1. Let _s4_ be _string_ characters from *24* to *35*. 1. NOTE: Canonicalizes Bluetooth Base UUID. 1. If _s1_ is *"0000"* and _s2_ is *"1000"* and _s3_ is *"8000"* and _s4_ is *"00805f9b34fb"*, then 1. If _s0_ starts with *"0000"*, then 1. Let _string_ be _s0_ characters from index *4* to *7*. 1. Else, 1. Let _string_ be _s0_. 1. Else, 1. Let _string_ be _s0_ *+* _s1_ *+* _s2_ *+* _s3_ *+* _s4_. 1. Else if _n_ is neither *4* nor *8*, then 1. Throw. 1. [declared="Uint8Array"] Let _view_ be Call(GetProperty(_Uint8Array_, "*fromHex*"), _Uint8Array_, _string_). 1. Return GetProperty(_view_, "*buffer*").
